title: cannot access /dev/fd/1 from UID!=0
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
When trying to run supervisord inside a container with podman and UID!=0 you get:
IOError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/dev/fd/1'

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. podman run image

**Describe the results you received:**
https://gist.github.com/thoraxe/0139d451ba627b488683a83204ddf6e7

**Describe the results you expected:**
Image runs

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
https://github.com/openshift/openshift-cns-testdrive/blob/ocp4-dev/Dockerfile

This used to work in previous versions of Podman.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

comments.body: @rhatdan @giuseppe @vrothberg I feel like this happened before, but I can't seem to find details. Any ideas?

I was previously using F28 and it worked there, so whatever podman version was present in F28 as of a few months ago it worked. You can use quay.io/openshiftroadshow/lab-ocp-ocs:ocp4-prod to test with

this may have something to do with no tty being allocated...
`podman run --rm quay.io/openshiftroadshow/lab-ocp-ocs:ocp4-prod`
fails
`podman run --rm -t quay.io/openshiftroadshow/lab-ocp-ocs:ocp4-prod`
works (well for me)

had a bit more of a poke about on this, possibly a crun problem, think it's creating fifos with incorrect ownership.

with crun:

`podman run --rm --user 1000:1000 fedora:31 /bin/bash -c 'ls -lah /dev/fd/1; while true; do echo "test" > /dev/fd/1; sleep 2; done;'`
fails with permission denied, if i use lsof on the host to find the underlying fifo that's being used that fifo is owned uid 1000, the symlink to it is owned by 100999

with runc:

`podman run --rm --user 1000:1000 fedora:31 /bin/bash -c 'ls -lah /dev/fd/1; while true; do echo "test" > /dev/fd/1; sleep 2; done;'`
works without issue, if i use lsof on the host to find the underlying fifo that's being used that fifo is owned uid 100999 as is the symlink to it.

so guessing that somewhere in the crun code ownership is not being set correctly after the fifo is created

all of this is on f31, podman 1.8.0, conmon: conmon-2.0.10-2.fc31.x86_64, runc: runc-1.0.0-102.dev.gitdc9208a.fc31.x86_64, crun: crun-0.12.2.1-1.fc31.x86_64 and kernel: 5.5.7-200.fc31.x86_64
that should be fixed with conmon 2.0.11: https://github.com/containers/conmon/pull/112
@giuseppe @jnovy @lsm5 Looks like conmon-.2.0.11 is built for F31, but not in updates testing?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
we have an updated conmon for Fedora 31 now.  Closing the issue
